title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 21, 2026
headline: Humana's nH Predict AI denial system and a DOJ False Claims Act complaint alleging disability discrimination in Medicare Advantage enrollment drive a band-crossing proposal from Functional to Developing (-5.4), the June 2026 series' most methodologically consequential pending finding — while Sudan's RSF massing around El Obeid prompts a formal UN Security Council demand to halt and the UN rights chief's public call to 'stop this madness,' and Afghanistan's Taliban open fire on Herat women's-rights protesters, killing a child.
summary: June 21 is a single-proposal cycle across 20 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 1 band-crossing proposal (PENDING) and 19 confirmations with 7 floor/near-floor reinforcements. The lead finding is Humana (fortune-500): a proposed downgrade from 40.6 Functional to 35.2 Developing (-5.4), driven by two independent federal/judicial evidence channels — the DOJ False Claims Act complaint alleging Humana conspired to discriminate against disabled Medicare beneficiaries (Tier 5, May 2025) and the Barrows v. Humana AI-denial class action surviving a motion to dismiss (June 2026). The proposal is documented at the Developing-band level but held pending because pre-adjudication discipline cautions against crossing a band on unadjudicated evidence. The floor cluster accumulates its most severe simultaneous reinforcement of the June series: Afghanistan 0.0 (Taliban lethal force on women's-rights protesters, child killed), Sudan 0.0 (UNSC formal demand to halt RSF assault), xAI/Grok 0.0 (OPC Canada adjudicated privacy finding). Anthropic confirms at 59.1 on the victim/perpetrator rule: the Commerce Department export-control order is government action inflicted on Anthropic. DeepMind/Google confirms at 56.9 on its AI Control Roadmap.
